description_long: This bungalow features a broad, low-pitched hip roof covered in brown shingles, extending over a wraparound porch supported by slender posts. The exterior walls are constructed of reddish-brown brick, giving the structure a solid and traditional appearance. The front facade includes a concrete porch with a metal railing and a central entrance, flanked by a few windows. The side and rear elevations display a simple arrangement of rectangular windows, with the rear showing two evenly spaced windows and the side showing one. The foundation is made of exposed concrete, slightly elevating the building above ground level. The overall design is compact and single-storied, typical of suburban or neighborhood residential settings.
description: A single-story brick bungalow with a wide hip roof, wraparound porch, and simple rectangular windows. The structure is compact, with a concrete foundation and traditional residential features.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main entrance, wraparound porch with railing, and two windows on the facade.
description_view_1: Side view displaying a single window and the uninterrupted expanse of the hip roof.
description_view_2: Rear view with two symmetrically placed windows and the brick wall fully visible.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with two windows and a partial view of the porch structure.
attribute_window_arrangement: asymmetrical with single and paired rectangular windows on each facade
